boundary: This is a schema inventory only. It does not test a generator and does not close Gap A.
interpretation: This inventory checks whether the copied Project 18 source cache contains richer station provenance fields such as address_pair_source, columns, station keys, or BAC/column endpoint records. If promising files are thin or derived, the next step is to import the upstream raw WXYZTI station artifact from Project 18 or the source lab rather than continuing relation searches on thin audits.

needle_hits_first_80.sample: The witness is the nearest neighbor that answers back: the shared_B carrier preserves B/slot, while the paired reverse_partner row supplies the returned C value by swapping B and C.

The realized WXYZTI overlay has a paired local grammar. Each directed C-transition has one shared_B row and one reciprocal reverse_partner row. reverse_partner is the answering row: it preserves A and swaps B/C. shared_B preserves B/slot, and its target C is supplied by the paired reverse_partner row.
Generate the 12 reciprocal shared_B/reverse_partner pairs from a native candidate universe, rather than merely auditing realized WXYZTI rows.
shared_B's target C is not explained by a standalone shared_B formula. It is coupled to the reciprocal reverse_partner row whose from_B/from_slot supplies the answer.
reverse_partner preserves A, sends to_C = from_B = from_slot, and sends to_B/to_slot = from_C.
shared_B preserves B/slot. No additional global linear invariant involving A/C was found in the tested family.

The WXYZTI overlay has a strong local selector chain. Signed G15 native profile reduces the role-block circulation candidates to 7. Observed support, q0/q3 inclusion, and shared junction C=2 reduce those to 2. Directed WXYZTI channel compatibility selects exactly the target circulation.

This is a strong local selector chain for the WXYZTI role-block circulation. It shows that the target circulation is not arbitrary inside the signed G15/WXYZTI context. It is not, however, a G60-native generator derivation, because the directed WXYZTI transition table remains upstream.

Derive or independently source the directed WXYZTI transition table from WXYZTI station constraints, oriented G15/G60 structure, old Hyperxi/G60 involution shadows, or another native object not using the directed transition table as an assumption.
7 full native-profile matches among 25225200 valid ordered triples
This audits realized rows only. Exact rules here do not prove selection from non-realized candidate rows and do not close Gap A.
This searches for low-complexity deterministic station-provenance rules over the realized WXYZTI rows. The aim is to separate real compact laws from row-identity-like selectors.
This is still over realized rows only. A splitter here explains a realized ambiguity but does not prove selection from a non-realized candidate universe or close Gap A.
This is still a row-pair candidate universe built from realized WXYZTI rows. It is stronger than a realized-pair audit, but it is not a native G60 generator and does not close Gap A.
All shared_B rows crossed with all reverse_partner rows having the same role_pair.

The candidate universe is broader than the realized reciprocal pairs: every shared_B row is crossed with every reverse_partner row in the same role_pair. The strict pair grammar tests whether reciprocal answer coupling selects exactly the 12 realized pairs.
This is a coordinate-shadow audit over realized WXYZTI rows only. It does not prove a complete generator, does not derive G60-native structure, and does not close Gap A.
This audits realized rows only. It does not prove selection from non-realized candidates and does not close Gap A.
This isolates the clean reverse_partner law from the shared_B residual. It tests simple source-field laws for to_C across role classes and station roles.
This is an invariant audit over realized station rows only. It does not generate the allowed transitions from a non-realized candidate universe and does not close Gap A.
This audits preservation and equality invariants for reverse_partner and shared_B WXYZTI station rows. It tests whether reverse_partner and shared_B are distinguished by preserving A vs B/slot.
This is an exact law over realized WXYZTI station rows. It does not generate the allowed transitions from a larger candidate universe, does not derive G60-native structure, and does not close Gap A.

For realized WXYZTI rows, endpoints preserve the same 15-sheet and 30-sheet. The lift q is determined by ordinary integer C wrap: q=0 when to_C-from_C >= 0, q=3 when to_C-from_C < 0. The fiber delta is (to_C-from_C) mod 60.
This audits realized shared_B rows only. It does not generate the shared_B candidate set and does not close Gap A.
This searches for linear mod-15 invariants or constant-delta quantities under shared_B transforms on (A,B,C).
This is over realized rows only. Role-specific affine laws may be descriptive or overfit; they do not prove selection from non-realized candidates and do not close Gap A.
This searches the shared_B residual station roles IW, XY, and ZT for role-specific affine mod-15 laws for to_C. It separates source-side laws from laws that use target-side fields.
This audits realized reciprocal pairs only. It does not generate the pair set from a non-realized candidate universe and does not close Gap A.

This pairs each shared_B row with its reciprocal reverse_partner row using (role_pair, from_C, to_C). It tests whether shared_B's target is coupled to the neighboring reverse_partner row rather than explained by a standalone formula.
This is a law search over realized rows only. Affine laws found here do not prove selection from non-realized candidates and do not close Gap A.

This searches for simple affine mod-15 laws by station role, role pair, or role class. It is meant to test whether the allowed to_C values are generated by station-specific source-field formulas rather than arbitrary lookup.
This audits only realized station rows. It does not compare against non-realized candidate rows, does not prove a complete generator, and does not close Gap A.
This audits realized WXYZTI station rows for provenance laws. It checks field deltas, same-field constraints, and deterministic maps such as station_role+from_C -> to_C.
This audits realized station rows only. It does not generate shared_B from a non-realized candidate universe and does not close Gap A.

This writes the reverse_partner side as an explicit station transform and profiles the remaining shared_B transform by station role. reverse_partner should be exactly (A,B,C)->(A,C,B). shared_B preserves B/slot but has residual A/C motion.
This is a schema/feature inspection. It does not prove a generator and does not close Gap A.
This inspects WXYZTI station-row features to see whether expected directed C-transitions differ from nonexpected role-compatible C-pairs by station/provenance fields.
